channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes across Gaza kill at least 26, including 3 kids
item.description: Israeli airstrikes killed at least 26 people across the Gaza Strip on Thursday, including a strike in an Israeli-declared humanitarian zone that killed three children and two high-ranking police officers, according to Palestinian and hospital officials.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Puerto Rico's new governor sworn in days after a major blackout left much of the island in the dark
item.description: Jenniffer González Colón has been sworn in as Puerto Rico’s new governor as the island held for a normally ebullient ceremony overshadowed by widespread anger over a blackout that hit the U.S. territory days ago.
item.dc:creator: Dánica Coto, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: British rapper Stormzy banned from driving for using phone behind wheel of his Rolls-Royce
item.description: British rapper Stormzy has been banned from driving for nine months after admitting that he drove his Rolls-Royce through London while using his phone.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Montenegro mourns after gunman kills at least 12 people before shooting himself
item.description: Shock and dismay prevails in Montenegro after a gunman fatally shot 12 people, including two children, in a western town before killing himself.
item.dc:creator: Predrag Milic, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bangladesh court again rejects bail for Hindu leader who led rallies
item.description: A court in southeastern Bangladesh has rejected a plea for bail by a jailed Hindu leader who led large rallies in the Muslim-majority country demanding better security for minority groups.
item.dc:creator: Julhas Alam, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Jocelyne Wildenstein, Swiss socialite known for extreme plastic surgeries, dies at 84: reports
item.description: Wildenstein's facial surgeries were notably feline, leading the press to (cruelly) dub her "Catwoman.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope calls for commitment to protect life as he doubles down on abortion in New Year's Day message
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis ushered in the New Year with a renewed appeal for the faithful to reject abortion, calling for a “firm commitment” to protect and respect life from conception to natural death. Francis, 88, celebrated a New Year’s Day Mass in St.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea air crash investigators extract black box data as grieving families mourn the victims
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Grieving relatives of the victims of the South Korea plane crash gathered at the site to pay respects to their loved ones on New Year’s Day, as officials said they've extracted data from one of the retrieved black boxes to find the exact cause of the crash.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Biden speaks to Puerto Rico governor, Energy secretary about power outages
item.description: President Biden said he spoke with the governor of Puerto Rico and the Energy secretary after a blackout hit nearly all of the island early Tuesday morning. During their talk, Biden offered Puerto Rico Gov.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Trump, Putin advisers trade barbs heading into new year
item.description: The year is ending with a pair of sharp messages between top advisers to Russian leader Vladimir Putin and President-elect Trump. Trump’s choice for special envoy to Ukraine and Russia, retired Lt. Gen. Keith Kellogg, slammed a Russian missile and drone attack from last week.
item.dc:creator: Colin Meyn, The Hill

item.title: At least 66 people die after a truck plunges into a river in southern Ethiopia
item.description: At least 66 people were killed in southern Ethiopia after an overcrowded truck that was contracted by wedding guests plunged into a river.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Airline says pre-flight inspection of South Korea plane showed 'no issues': report
item.description: Investigators are in South Korea searching through plane crash debris to try to determine what caused the country's deadliest air disaster in decades, which killed 179.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Palestinian official predicts Trump will 'destroy' Iran, leading to breakdown of remaining Hamas cells: report
item.description: Palestinian official Mohammad Hamdan said he believes President-elect Donald Trump will "destroy Iran" and effectively decimate Hamas' grasp on the region.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Montenegro extradites cryptocurrency mogul Do Kwon to the United States
item.description: PODGORICA, Montenegro (AP) — Montenegro on Tuesday extradited a South Korean mogul known as “the cryptocurrency king” to the United States, following a decision of its justice ministry earlier this month to accept a U.S.
item.dc:creator: PREDRAG MILIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Longest-held Guantánamo Bay detainee sent back to Tunisia
item.description: A detainee at a military prison in Guantánamo Bay, Cuba, has been repatriated to Tunisia, the Pentagon announced Monday. Ridah Bin Saleh al-Yazidi, 59, was eligible to transfer following an interagency review process, the release said. He was the longest-held detainee at the prison.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, The Hill

item.title: Arrest warrant issued for impeached South Korean president as political crisis deepens
item.description: A South Korean court approved arrest warrants on Tuesday to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol and search his office and residence amid a criminal investigation into his martial law decree.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Ukraine: How the war shifted in 2024
item.description: A third year of war in Ukraine drags on as the new year and the conflict's February anniversary approach. The war in 2024 saw major shifts on the battlefield and in Western politics.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean court issues warrants to detain impeached President Yoon and search his office
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — A South Korean court issued warrants Tuesday to detain impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol and search his office and residence over allegations of rebellion in connection with his short-lived declaration of martial law.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Politician among protesters arrested in Kenya while calling for an end to alleged abductions
item.description: NAIROBI, Kenya (AP) — An opposition politician and several other protesters were arrested in Kenya on Monday during street demonstrations calling for an end to alleged abductions, that recently targeted young government critics.
item.dc:creator: EVELYNE MUSAMBI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Putin offers to pay off debts as recruitment tool in war against Ukraine
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin signed a bill into law last month that offers to pay off up to $96,000 in debt in exchange for military service.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hundreds of soldiers freed in the latest prisoner exchange between Russia and Ukraine
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russia and Ukraine have exchanged prisoners of war in the latest such swap that saw the release of hundreds of captives and was brokered with the help of the United Arab Emirates, officials said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis kicks off a yearlong Jubilee that will test his stamina and Rome's patience
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis kicked off the 2025 Holy Year on Tuesday, inaugurating a celebration of the Catholic Church that is expected to draw some 32 million pilgrims to Rome in a test of the pope's stamina and the ability of the Eternal City to welcome them.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: World population will be 8.09 billion on New Year's Day after 71 million increase in 2024
item.description: The world population increased by more than 71 million people in 2024 and will be 8.09 billion people on New Year's Day, according to U.S. Census Bureau estimates released Monday.
item.dc:creator: Mike Schneider, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Israeli spy network uncovers Hezbollah commander's plans to marry his 4 mistresses
item.description: Israeli spy agencies infiltrated Hezbollah so successfully that they learned intimate details of commander Fuad Shukr's plans to marry his four mistresses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Uncovering the atrocities of the Assad regime at its 'death factory' on the hill
item.description: Sednaya prison was the site where thousands of Syrians were tortured and killed under Assad’s regime. Investigators have uncovered mass graves, and are calling for justice.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US sends Ukraine final Biden-approved weapons, financial package
item.description: The Biden administration's final aid to Ukraine before Donald Trump takes office is a $1.25 billion drawdown package.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Azerbaijan's president says crashed jetliner was shot down by Russia unintentionally
item.description: Azerbaijan’s President Ilham Aliyev said Sunday that the Azerbaijani airliner that crashed last week was shot down by Russia, albeit unintentionally, and criticized Moscow for trying to “hush up” the issue for days. "We can say with complete clarity that the plane was shot down by Russia.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea plane's final moments captured on video before hitting concrete barrier, triggering explosion
item.description: A video released Sunday shows the exact moment a plane skidded off a runway in South Korea before crashing into a concrete barrier, causing an explosion.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Plane skids off runway in South Korea, killing at least 177
item.description: A passenger plane burst into flames Sunday after it skidded off a runway at a South Korean airport and slammed into a concrete fence when its front landing gear apparently failed to deploy.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Putin apologizes for 'tragic incident' but stops short of saying Azerbaijani plane was shot down
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Saturday apologized to his Azerbaijani counterpart for what he called a “tragic incident” following the crash of an Azerbaijani airliner in Kazakhstan that killed 38 people, but stopped short of acknowledging that Moscow was responsible.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: China sanctions 7 companies over US military assistance to Taiwan
item.description: The sanctions also come in response to the recent approval of the U.S. government's annual defense spending bill, which a Chinese Foreign Ministry statement said “includes multiple negative sections on China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation
